steps.title: Identify the slope of line j
steps.content: From the graph, line $k$ has slope $-\frac{4}{3}$. Since line $j$ is perpendicular to line $k$, line $j$'s slope is the negative reciprocal: $m = \frac{3}{4}$.

steps.title: Write the equation for line j using point-slope form
steps.content:
With slope $\frac{3}{4}$ through $(-20, -23)$, point-slope form gives:
$$y + 23 = \frac{3}{4}(x + 20)$$
Distribute and simplify:
$$y = \frac{3}{4}x + 15 - 23 = \frac{3}{4}x - 8$$

steps.title: Match the equation to the choices
steps.content: The simplified equation $y = \frac{3}{4}x - 8$ matches choice A. That matches choice A — A is correct.
